title: Nernst Equation Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactive Nernst calculator with galvanic cell diagram, live Q updates, and an E vs log Q graph.

learningObjectives: Use the Nernst equation to compute cell potential under non-standard concentrations.
Relate the sign/magnitude of E to the reaction quotient Q and electron count n.

keyQuestions: How does Q change when the oxidized vs reduced concentrations change?
Why does increasing n flatten the E vs log Q line?
commonMisconceptions: Q should be built from separate half-reactions instead of the overall reaction.
Increasing both concentrations equally changes E.
assessmentOpportunities: Students screenshot the substituted Nernst equation and explain each number.
Learners tune the sliders until E switches sign and describe the concentration relationship.
